structural_claim: Hierarchical structure formation through gravitational/wave instabilities creates networks where local perturbations grow into global patterns determined by the statistics of initial fluctuations

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Dark matter halos form hierarchically through gravitational collapse of density peaks in a Gaussian random field
Gravitational instability naturally creates a web-like network topology where matter flows along filaments into nodes
Disorder-induced interference creates localized structures from extended wave states through statistical processes
